answers.answer: `status=resolved` should remain a standalone way to request resolved markets. The current backend only models a `status` filter, `GET /v1/markets?status=resolved` is already frozen to return `m3`, and the Market List UI plus frontend client/hooks only send `status`. Requiring `include_resolved=true` as a second knob would break that existing path. For the contradictory case `status=resolved&include_resolved=false`, the codebase has no direct precedent, but normalizing the effective mode so resolved markets are still returned matches the preserved `status=resolved` contract better than returning an empty list.
answers.uncertainty: There is no current test or handler branch for the explicit contradictory combination `status=resolved&include_resolved=false`.

answers.answer: `include_resolved` should follow the endpoint's existing strict query-parameter contract: accept at most one value, default a missing parameter to `false`, preserve blanks as invalid input because `/v1/markets` uses `parse_qs(..., keep_blank_values=True)`, and return `400 invalid_query` with `details.parameter="include_resolved"` plus `details.received` echoing the raw value or duplicate list. There is no HTTP boolean-query parser today; the only permissive boolean parser is `read_bool_env(...)`, which is explicitly for environment variables. For public HTTP queries, the established style is stricter single-value validation, so `true` and `false` only is the best fit.
answers.uncertainty: The exact accepted boolean literals are inferred from the server's existing query-validation style because no HTTP boolean query param exists yet.

answers.answer: If any frontend surface needs to preserve the old all-markets view after the backend default changes, `include_resolved` cannot stay backend-only. The shared collection surface currently only accepts `status`: `listMarkets(status?)`, `queryKeys.markets(status?)`, and `useMarkets(status?)`. Backend tests also freeze `meta.filters`, while the frontend list response is typed with a generic `Meta` shape that does not model list filters at all. I did not find a separate `MarketListFilters` or search-param helper abstraction in this repo, so the practical contract surface is backend `meta.filters`, the API client signature, the query-key/hook signature, and the tests that pin those shapes.
answers.uncertainty: A backend-only patch is still technically possible if the product decision is that no caller beyond `status=resolved` ever needs to opt back into resolved markets.

answers.answer: `System` is the clearest default opt-in candidate because it derives total/resolved counts and aggregate volume/liquidity from an unfiltered `useMarkets()` call, so hiding resolved rows would undercount that screen. The market-detail graph is the other strong opt-in candidate: `MarketDetail` always renders `BayesNetGraph`, but the graph currently loads the default market collection, so a resolved detail page can lose its focus node if resolved markets disappear from the default list. `AssumptionPanel` and `ConditionalEditor` already immediately narrow their collections to active markets, so they do not need opt-in. `Portfolio` only uses the collection for title lookup, and account exposure rows are pruned on resolution, so it can likely stay on the new default. `MarketList` itself should keep resolved markets hidden by default and rely on `status=resolved` when the user explicitly requests them.
answers.uncertainty: The remaining product choice is whether the graph should always opt in or only do so when the focused market is resolved; there is also no separate `Analytics` route in this checkout.

assumption_checks.impact: The list surface only distinguishes resolved markets through `status`, and the seeded resolved fixture `m3` uses `status == "resolved"`, so filtering on `market["status"]` is sufficient for this task.

Default `GET /v1/markets` currently includes the resolved seeded market, and backend tests freeze both the count (`3`) and the exact `meta.filters` shape. Changing the default is a real contract change.

Several frontend surfaces call `useMarkets()` with no explicit filter today, so a backend default change will alter those screens unless they either opt back in or already narrow to active markets themselves.

The work is unlikely to stay purely localized to backend parsing/filtering if any screen needs to preserve the old all-markets behavior. At minimum, the client/hook/query-key contract and some route behavior would need coordinated updates.
